description:
**Milestone:** Phase 2: Component Expansion

Build hx-nav: primary and secondary navigation component. Supports horizontal menu bar and vertical sidebar patterns. Mobile responsive with hamburger toggle.

**Files to Modify:**
- packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-nav/

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Horizontal and vertical layouts
- [ ] Nested submenu support
- [ ] Mobile responsive with hamburger toggle
- [ ] Active/current page indication
- [ ] CSS parts: nav, list, item, link, toggle
- [ ] Keyboard: arrow keys, Enter, Escape for submenus
- [ ] ARIA: role=navigation, aria-current, aria-expanded for submenus
- [ ] <5KB gzipped

**Complexity:** large
title: Implement hx-nav component

coderabbitai: **Actionable comments posted: 6**

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

Inline comments:
In `@packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-nav/hx-nav.styles.ts`:
- Around line 4-239: Replace all hardcoded color, spacing, typography and timing
literals in this stylesheet with the project's design tokens: swap hex/color
fallbacks in [part='nav'], [part='toggle'], .nav__link, .nav__link--active,
.nav__submenu, :host([orientation='vertical']) rules, etc., to use --hx- color
tokens (e.g. --hx-nav-bg, --hx-nav-color, --hx-nav-link-hover-bg,
--hx-nav-link-active-bg, --hx-nav-submenu-bg, etc.), replace rem/fallbacks for
font-size, padding, gap and min-width with --hx- spacing/typography tokens (e.g.
--hx-nav-padding, --hx-nav-item-padding, --hx-space-*, --hx-font-size-*), and
replace hardcoded durations (150ms, 200ms) in transition rules for .nav__link,
.nav__chevron and [part='toggle'] with timing tokens like
--hx-transition-duration-small/medium; update any other literals (border-radius,
box-shadow offsets) to corresponding --hx- tokens so every literal is
token-driven.

In `@packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-nav/hx-nav.ts`:
- Around line 126-131: The keydown handler _handleKeydown is selecting all
'[part="link"]' including hidden submenu links, so the incoming top-level index
no longer matches itemsArr; change the query to only select top-level links
(e.g., scope to immediate children of the nav item container or filter links
whose closest('[part="item"]') is a top-level item and not inside a
submenu/hidden container), then rebuild itemsArr from that filtered list so
current = itemsArr[index] matches the top-level index; apply the same scoping
change to the other keyboard-navigation selection logic referenced around the
second occurrence (the code at the other handler using
querySelectorAll('[part="link"]')).
- Around line 216-224: The documen
statusChangeReason: PR #124 created after rebase onto dev. Ready for CI.

threadFeedback.agentReasoning: Line 239 is the closing of the `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block. The block only sets `transition: none` but doesn't include `animation: none`. Future animations added to the component would not be suppressed. Adding `animation: none` provides proper WCAG 2.3.3 compliance and is a genuine improvement.

Line 54 is the class definition start. The class declares `private _focusableItems: HTMLElement[] = []` at line 86 which is never populated or read anywhere — pure dead code. This violates the project's "No orphaned code" rule. Removing it is a clear improvement.

Line 131: `querySelectorAll('[part="link"]')` selects ALL link elements including those inside submenus. When a submenu is open, the index arithmetic is wrong — `itemsArr[index]` won't correspond to the correct top-level nav item. This is a real keyboard navigation bug. The selector must be scoped to top-level nav item links only.

CRITICAL: Line 218 adds `this._handleOutsideClick.bind(this)` as a listener. Line 224 calls `removeEventListener` with a DIFFERENT bound function (`.bind()` creates a new function object each call). The listener is never actually removed, causing a memory leak and stale event handler accumulation each time the component reconnects. This is a definite bug.

Line 319: `item.children!` uses a non-null assertion which explicitly violates the project's zero-tolerance policy ("No non-null assertions"). The `hasChildren` guard on line 297 already guarantees `item.children` is defined when this branch executes, so the fix is trivial: use `item.children ?? []`.
